council.headline: The rebalance executed and reconciled, and the fund was mutated under the council’s approved target-change decision.
council.riskNotes: The main risk was the mixed fresh-and-aggregate evidence on the preferred add, which lowered conviction relative to a purely fresh-confirmed candidate.
council.targetFinalization: Target finalization confirmed the ratified posture and produced a certified target structure for execution.
council.actionItems: The session closed as resolved and completed, with the fund mutated, reconciled, and handed off under the approved target-change decision.

macro.summary: The Effective Score of 32 (on 0–100) signals a **Risk-Off regime (R-)** with material headwinds offsetting modest tailwinds. Global liquidity expansion and a normalized yield curve provide a base of support, but Bitcoin spot ETF outflows extending into a seventh consecutive day, combined with geopolitical escalation (US military strikes on Iran) and regulatory tightening (Spain's MiCA stance, EU DeFi oversight push), have compressed institutional conviction and forced a 10.95-point qualitative downgrade from the base score of 43. This matters now because crypto is caught between long-term tokenization momentum (Securitize's NYSE listing) and immediate deleveraging pressure, leaving retail portfolios vulnerable to sharp intra-week swings.

signalExample.name: Defactor
signalExample.body: Appeared in 7/7 daily runs across messyinfra and base01 and base02 over the 7-day lookback. Appeared in 7/7 source run(s) with avg rank 2.86 and best rank 1.

weeklyHighlights: Macro currently reads R- (32.0/100). The score updates daily; each council session uses the latest macro read available at meeting time.
base01 held 5 council sessions this week — 3 executed rotations on Base and 2 hold reviews.
Decentralized Finance (DeFi) leads narrative momentum (+10.7% over 15 days) — thematic heat, not a buy signal on its own.
REAL showed up consistently across fund screens this week — a leading aggregate candidate, not an automatic trade.

readerNotes.text: Fund pages in the app now include full council minutes — open any fund and tap Council to read what the portfolio committee debated, voted on, and whether trades were executed.

link.label: How the AI investment council works

readerNotes.text: We publish two live workflow test funds (base01 and base02) alongside the read-only Guru Lotus snapshots. Every change on those test funds goes through screening, council review, and signed execution — the same path future AI-managed funds will use.
